scope: The @spec-engine/tracker package: the offline-default adapter and the optional Linear read integration that resolves an opaque issue_id to a title/URL for display. Belongs here: the adapter interface + offline noop adapter; the single read query to the tracker; the SPEC_TRACKER_TOKEN auth + no-token short-circuit; the sidecar cache; the engine-isolation boundary (engine internals never import the tracker; no external network from the derivation path). Does not belong here: the provenance matrix + issue-id opacity model itself (→ PROV); any write back to the tracker (forbidden — the integration is one-way/read-only). Boundary case: resolving an issue_id to a title is a TRK concern, but the opacity of that id inside the engine's index stays a PROV promise.

requirements.statement: The spec engine internals never import @spec-engine/tracker and emit no external-network host literal, so the derived index is built fully offline.
requirements.why: If the derivation path reached the network or the tracker, spec index and spec check could hang, leak, or vary by network state — breaking the offline-deterministic guarantee adopters depend on.

requirements.statement: The tracker adapter sends only a GraphQL read query to the issue tracker — never a mutation — so requirement state is looked up but never written back (one-way truth).
requirements.why: A mutation path would let the tool modify tracker state as a side effect of a read — turning a lookup into an unaudited write and violating the one-way-truth boundary.

requirements.statement: The tracker adapter reads SPEC_TRACKER_TOKEN once from the environment, sends it only as the raw Authorization header, and never writes it to a console.* call, so the credential cannot leak into logs.
requirements.why: A token written to a log line would leak a live credential into CI output or a developer's terminal scrollback — a credential-exposure incident from a debug print.
